posts.caption:
Lois Weber, Hypocrites (1915) “a morality tale that finds parallels between the medieval period and 1910s America. Weber takes aim at the clergy, politicians, and the ruling class over 100 years ago. Margaret Edwards plays Truth, and she appears nude throughout a dream sequence, with Weber photographing her in double exposure, giving Truth a ghostly appearance.”
*
Germaine Dulac, The Smiling Madame Beudet, (1922) A complex, expressionist film about a troubled marriage includes daydreams that represent the protagonist’s inner world, in which she drives away in her own motorcar, or unsettling visions where her husband crawls in through a window.
*
Elvira Notari, a pioneer of Italian silent cinema (1875–1946), was renowned for her realistic, gritty Neapolitan melodramas, many of which featured poetic, subjective sequences that operated as dreams, memories, or internal fantasies rather than straight narrative.
*
✨Women were incredibly influential in the silent film era as directors, writers, producers, and editors ✨ let’s remember to give them their flowers too.

posts.caption:
@indyamoore’s performance in Father Mother Sister Brother shimmers with warm authenticity. ⁠
⁠
Jim Jarmusch’s 14th feature is a tight triptych of unconnected stories that come together to say something profound about family ties. In the third vignette, Moore – a former AnOther Magazine cover star – and Luka Sabbat play a sister and brother who return to their childhood home in Paris after the death of their parents. Though their predicament is sadder than the film’s other characters, theirs is the most hopeful story. Moore’s Skye and Sabbat’s Billy no longer live in one another’s pockets, but their sibling bond has endured. ⁠
⁠
Moore, who broke through in 2018 by playing resilient sex worker Angel Vasquez-Evangelista in the groundbreaking LGBTQ+ series Pose, channelled their own grief into Skye’s. While they were filming Father Mother Sister Brother in early 2024, they were also using their profile to draw attention to the plight of genocide survivors in Palestine. “I was interfacing every single day with people who were trying to evacuate Gaza,” Moore says. “I was seeing images of people’s wounds, of children who were severely hurt, and I had to reckon with the weight of that on my body.” ⁠
⁠
At the link in bio, they speak candidly to @misternicklevine about acting, biotechnology, and serving as an advocate for causes including Palestine, the dismantling of white supremacy, and LGBTQ+ rights 📲⁠
⁠
📸 Father Mother Sister Brother, 2026. Photography by Yorick Le Saux⁠
⁠
#IndyaMoore
posts.alt_text: Photo by AnOther Magazine on April 15, 2026. May be an image of one or more people, makeup, people standing, magazine and text that says 'AnOther Indya Moore on Grief, Family Ties and Activism'.

posts.caption:
In New Genesis, his second book following Rotting from Within, photographer @kircherabdul continues his intimate exploration of human experience, this time documenting the life of Sierra Kiss, a single mother navigating a precarious existence in Los Angeles. ⁠
⁠
Published by @jointsloose and consisting of 144 pages, the photo book exposes the frailties of the systems meant to support vulnerable populations – shelters, churches, and social services, all eroded by chronic underfunding and restrictive policies, leaving individuals like Sierra to fend for themselves. Through Kircher’s lens, these structural failures are not abstract statistics – they are lived realities, marked by personal, intimate moments of crisis, fleeting relief, and Sierra’s daily life.⁠
⁠
At the link in bio, Kircher speaks with @karmmaacoma about capturing the daily rhythm of Sierra Kiss's life 📲⁠
⁠
📸 New Genesis © @kircherabdul, 2026. Courtesy of @jointsloose
posts.alt_text: Photo by AnOther Magazine on April 13, 2026. May be an image of child, magazine, poster and text that says 'AnOther Abdulhamid Kircher's Unflinching Portrait of a Single Mother in Los Angeles'.

posts.caption:
There’s a particular kind of designer who insists their work is not about them. Then there’s womenswear designer @fidannovruzovastudio, who is refreshingly honest about the opposite. “I design by projecting my own needs,” she smiles.⁠
⁠
The key reference point of her Autumn/Winter 2026 collection is Art Deco portrait artist Tamara de Lempicka, and the idea that she painted her muses as versions of herself. “They look like themselves, but they also look like her,” Novruzova explains. “I quite resonated with the idea of an auto-portrait of a muse.” The campaign, launching exclusively with AnOther, pushes that idea further.⁠
⁠
At the link in bio, the designer speaks with @georgepistachio about making clothes with an emphasis on how they function in real life. Read more at the link in bio 📲⁠
⁠

posts.caption:
Between walking runways and fronting campaigns internationally to her silver screen debut in ‘Anyone but You’, @charleefraser is a multi-talented woman. But among Fraser’s manifold skills is one talent that she has, until now, kept on the quiet. “I love roller skating,” she shares. “I’m constantly thinking with the work that I do and [my] schedule. I’m a major organiser – such a Capricorn,” she laughs. “[With] my skates on, the only thing I can think about is what I’m doing. And it just really grounds me, brings me back into that present.”
Through the link in bio, see Charlee Fraser photographed at sunrise on the Northern Beaches of Sydney for BAZAAR.
Charlee Fraser wears @ralphlauren Collection in the April 2026 issue of Harper’s BAZAAR Australia/New Zealand.

posts.caption:
“Oh, man, little baby Charlee,” @charleefraser chuckles as she reflects on her career beginnings. “It was really interesting because I wasn’t looking to model at the time – the opportunity kind of presented itself,” she says of her beginning. By 2016, Fraser had been modelling locally for three years, with early gigs including runway appearances for David Jones, Carla Zampatti and Bec + Bridge. “My agency was like, ‘I think it’s time for you to go overseas. You’ve done everything here. Go spread the wings’,” she says. “At the time, I really felt ready for that.”
Continue reading Charlee Fraser’s interview with BAZAAR at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
For #BazaarIndia’s 17th anniversary issue, actor Ananya Panday (@AnanyaPanday) shares that she loves being alone now. “I used to be someone who couldn’t be alone at all. I’d be scared to sit alone for even 10 minutes. I always needed someone around me — my partner, my parents, my friends,” admits Panday. “But when I started living alone, even though I’m in the same building as my parents, that little bit of space changed everything. Building your own environment and doing things your own way has shifted my love for space.”
Much like our cover installation, which conveys the negotiation of the fragile space between two people, the artwork by How Are You Feeling Studio features two film cranes facing each other like figures. Their machine bodies, covered in soft fabric, suggest an unexpected intimacy.
Read the full cover story at the link in bio.

Anand Malligavad (@anandmalligavad) saw possibilities where others saw neglect. From reviving water bodies to rebuilding biodiversity, his work goes far beyond surface-level change—it’s about bringing entire ecosystems back to life.
The impact speaks for itself- healthier lakes, richer biodiversity, and communities that begin to care.
Because real change isn’t instant.
It’s built, sustained, and protected—over time.

Refillable lipsticks, fragrance bottles designed for topping up, metal compacts, and glass jars that stay on your shelf for years. The new luxury vanity isn’t built to be replaced; it’s designed to be kept. The mirrored cases of Rouge G from Guerlain (@guerlain) are designed to stay in circulation, while powder compacts like the Airbrush Flawless Finish from Charlotte Tilbury (@charlottetilbury) come with refill pans, allowing the signature rose-gold case to remain untouched.  Refillable beauty does not ask you to buy less. It asks you to hold on longer.
Tap the link in bio to read on.
✍️: Anoushka Madan
📸: Brands

posts.caption:
This week in fashion: Kering’s strategy overhaul; The return of Barneys — plus other key industry moves.
1. US health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said health regulators will ease rules on peptides, sparking a rally for wellness companies like Hims & Hers.
2. @AmericanEagle shares jumped after the retailer unveiled its second campaign featuring @Sydney_Sweeney, a year after an initial campaign that fuelled sales but polarised consumers.
3. @WMag announced the launch of @WYouth, a biannual print title for Gen Z readers helmed by Ava Nirui (@avanope) and editor-in-chief @SaraMoonves.
4. @DolceGabbana's leveraged business model has allowed the label to remain independent but is being tested by the luxury slowdown. Co-founder Stefano Gabbana is reportedly considering selling his 40 percent stake.
5. Licensing giant Authentic Brands Group is bringing @BarneysNY back to life at its previous flagship location at 660 Madison Avenue, sources said. Will it be the same?
6. @HalfMagicBeauty, the cosmetics brand from Euphoria makeup department head @Donni.Davy, debuted a Euphoria line to coincide with the premiere of the show’s third season, which has been widely panned.
7. @Kering_Official stock fell after the company detailed plans to double profit and close more @Gucci stores, but failed to provide revenue guidance beyond a vague promise to outperform the market.
8. @Hermès shares suffered a record plunge as Middle East sales slow and investors weigh the threat of overexposure, akin to what @LouisVuitton experienced in the early 2010s.
9. @Allbirds announced a head-scratching pivot from wool sneakers to AI, inviting suspicions that it’s all a ploy to chase tech hype and buoy its stock.
This Week in Fashion is BoF’s weekly news briefing, available exclusively to BoF Professional members. Become a BoF Professional member to access the full analysis #linkinbio
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on April 17, 2026. May be a graphic of leather, magazine, poster and text.

posts.caption: The real issue with luxury today isn't just the financial loss it's the loss of 'Prestige.' You can't claim scarcity while opening stores on every corner. Brands need to go back to Salon-style presentations. We don't need more 'noise' for an audience that doesn't buy; we need the 'Dream' back. Give seats to the tailors, the students, and the actual connoisseurs. That’s how you recreate the 'X Value' and bring back the mystery that mass-market strategies have killed. Experiences over expansions that’s the only way forward My proposal to return to Salons is not about exclusion; it is about justice for fashion. I want the student to sit beside the tailor and the designer, to live a true artistic experience away from the media circus , Fashion is a right for everyone who appreciates art, and the audience deserves an authentic 'dream experience,' not just a parade of soul-less branches. The Salon strategy might reduce profits 'quantitatively', but it increases them 'qualitatively' and rescues the brand's name from collapse.
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on April 17, 2026. May be an image of leather, magazine and text that says 'BOF C HIGH MARGIN MARGIN Kering S Strategy Reveal, Examined ROBERT WILLIAMS'.

posts.caption:
Gap is on a roll. In the last year, the brand has released hit campaign after hit campaign, such as a viral ad with K-Pop group Katseye, a spring campaign starring actor Parker Posey — timed perfectly with her White Lotus starring role — and a string of collaborations with buzzy labels such Sandy Liang, Cult Gaia, Beis and MadHappy.
Creative director Zac Posen has also brought Gap into Hollywood’s orbit, dressing stars from Timothée Chalamet to Anne Hathaway in the elevated GapStudio line. That momentum started showing up in the numbers: Gap Inc swung from losses in 2022 to net income of $844 million in fiscal year 2024, and the namesake Gap brand led recent results, with 2025 fourth quarter sales up 8 percent.
But creating brand heat is not the same as sustaining it. The question is, can Gap keep it up? Read the latest analysis by BoF Insights #linkinbio
✍️ @michellewiles_

Net Income Fiscal years 2019-2024, USD (millions) 844 -351 502 256 -202 -665 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 Source: Bloomberg data analysed going back to 1989 BoF INSIGHTS'.

posts.caption:
@Hermès sales missed analyst expectations this week, dragging shares down to their lowest level in over three years. The company cited disruption from the Middle East war, including lower traffic from Middle Eastern shoppers to stores in Italy, Switzerland and the UK. But sales in the critical Asia Pacific region also suffered. Is there something deeper going on?
There could be several explanations for Hermès’ lost momentum, but our diagnosis is leaning to the benign, with a temporary blip largely due to consumer distraction, more difficult comparables, and — possibly — tactical use of inventory.
That said, we see parallels between Hermès today and Louis Vuitton in the early-2010s and believe Hermès may soon face its own “Capucine moment.” At the time, Vuitton experienced a period of slowing growth, as the most sophisticated of luxury consumers grew too familiar with the brand and went elsewhere. Vuitton responded by stretching upwards, introducing the higher-end Capucine bag, proving that the sky is the limit for a top-tier luxury label.
We continue to believe in the fundamental building blocks of the Hermès brand. Yet, we also believe that Hermès has the potential to do much more.
Read the full report by Luca Solca #linkinbio
📷 Spotlight/Launchmetrics.com
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on April 16, 2026. May be an image of magazine, poster and text that says 'BOF What's Happening at Hermès? The French leather goods powerhouse is losing momentum and may soon face its own 'Capucine moment, drawing a comparison with Louis Vuitton in the early 2010s BY LUCA SOLCA'.

posts.caption:
When Elliot Hill returned to Nike as chief executive in October 2024, he was tasked with reversing one of the most significant slumps in the company’s history.
The business had lost momentum with both investors and consumers and his strategy has focused on restoring wholesale relationships, rebuilding key categories like running and trying to stabilise the brand’s broader narrative.
But Nike’s latest earnings and weak outlook have intensified doubts about whether the recovery is moving quickly enough. In a fragmented marketplace where heat has moved toward niche competitors and rejuvenated legacy rivals, Nike is struggling to convince a skeptical public and an impatient Wall Street that its next chapter has truly begun.
On this week’s episode of The Debrief, Sykes (@mikedsykes) joins hosts Sheena Butler-Young (@sheena.butler.young) and Brian Baskin (@bbaskin512) to unpack why Nike’s comeback still feels unfinished, what the brand is getting right, and what it would take for the market to believe again.
🎙️ Listen now #linkinbio
📷 Nike
posts.alt_text: Photo by The Business of Fashion on April 16, 2026. May be an image of toecap shoes, magazine, sneakers, sports equipment, sportswear and text that says 'BOF The Debrief DebriefPodcast Podcast Nike's Reality Check'.

posts.caption:
Kering aims to more than double its operating margin to over 22 percent in the mid-term as part of a wider strategic overhaul by CEO Luca de Meo. The revamp includes a new group-level platform to enhance “speed and efficiency” across functions including industry, client relations and technology.
At troubled flagship Gucci, the company will focus on reigniting desirability, reshaping product architecture and boosting quality. “Gucci is not vanilla ice cream. It is spicy,” De Meo said. “The essence of the brand is to feel good, attractive, optimistic, upbeat. It’s a feeling, not just a logo.”
Read the full story #linkinbio
✍️ Robert Williams (@robert.williams.writes)
📷 Getty Images

posts.caption:
@katieholmes had not one but two major reunions at a film screening in New York.
The first was with her original on-screen love interest from "Dawson's Creek," Joshua Jackson (@vancityjax), sending every Joey and Pacey shipper hurtling back in time nearly three decades. The second was a small but stylish detail that made heads turn in 2019: a slouchy shoulder with a visible bra strap.
Holmes' cashmere @khaite bra and bralette moment, which went unexpectedly viral seven years ago, received a red-carpet update this time around, with a white button-down styled off the shoulder, peekaboo strap, and a sequined charcoal skirt. She wore the custom @brunellocucinelli_brand look to support a film about the Italian designer, "Brunello: The Gracious Visionary," posing for pictures with Jackson ahead of the event.
📸 : Lexie Moreland/WWD/Getty Images/TheStewartofNY/GC Images/
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by CNN Style on April 16, 2026 tagging @brunellocucinelli, and @katieholmes. May be an image of text.

posts.caption:
This spring, it's all about tradwife with a twist. For a recording of "Live with Kelly and Mark" in New York on Wednesday, @ellefanning wore a girlish iridescent tutu and a buttery yellow polo shirt. From the waist down, she was dressed in her shimmering Sunday best.
From the waist up, the look was slouchy and boy-ish — like an off-duty tennis player, or someone's golf-loving father. The worlds-colliding look was designed by Paris-based brand August Barron, helmed by August Vestbø and Benjamin Barron, who subvert feminine and housewife aesthetics through unexpected layering, silhouettes and cutouts. Or often just by adding big black leather boots.
Fanning is currently promoting the Apple TV show "Margo's Got Money Troubles," and so far her outfits for the press tour have largely followed this line: slouchy, nostalgic and at times ironic versions of femininity. For her "Today" show appearance, Fanning wore the opening look from @rabanne Fall-Winter 2026 collection, which was inspired by tea dresses from the 1940s. The pinafore-style dress was in a twee ditsy print and flowed well past her knees, while a leather belt sat happily on her waist without exerting effort to cinch. On Monday, Fanning slipped into an equally vintage-inspired @albertaferretti rose-colored satin dress, covered in even more florals, with a built-in neckerchief.
📸: XNY/Star Max/GC Images/Getty Images; Cindy Ord/Getty Images; Jose Perez/Bauer-Griffin/GC Images/Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by CNN Style on April 09, 2026 tagging @ellefanning. May be an image of one or more people, people standing, newspaper, magazine, poster and text that says 'ELLE FANNING MAKES THE CASE FOR SLOUCHY, OLD-SCHOOL FEMININITY CNN'.

posts.caption:
A piece of the Parisian landmark's original staircase is going up for auction in the French capital next month.
There are a couple of catches, however: Bidders will need deep pockets to buy it and high ceilings to store it.
Section no. 1 of the @toureiffelofficielle's original spiral staircase connected the second and third floors of the tower when it opened in 1889. It comprises of 14 steps and measures 9ft high.
According to a listing on @artcurial__'s website, it is expected to fetch between $140,000 and $175,000.
"It has never been outside and underwent a complete restoration for the sale," Sabrina Dolla, associate director at Artcurial, told CNN. "It's an immersive experience, a stationary journey through time and space. Imagine yourself in 1889 on this staircase, perched between 113 and 276 meters high, with no safety barriers, but with a 360-degree view of Paris."
📸 : saiko3p/iStockphoto/Getty Images
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by CNN Style on April 09, 2026 tagging @toureiffelofficielle, and @artcurial__. May be an image of Sacré-Cœur, tower and text that says 'A PIECE OF THE EIFFEL TOWER IS BEING AUCTIONED - YOU'LL NEED DEEP POCKETS CNN'.

posts.caption:
Was there a dress code at the @euphoria season 3 premiere?
Across the desert-style red carpet we saw corseted hot pants, sports-mode sunglasses, vintage gowns, T-shirts, tube tops, sequinned mini-dresses and metallic bustiers. It was a fashion hodge podge that skewed more toward what one might wear to a festival — which made sense, given HBO Max is set to screen the first episode of the new season at @coachella on Sunday.
Even the main cast were on different sartorial wavelengths. @alexademie made her red carpet return in an ultra glam vintage '90s @muglerofficial gown, @zendaya was snapped in an slinky @ashistudio dress and @sydney_sweeney arrived in a white a '60s-style archival cape mini dress from @pierrecardin. @hunterschafer wore a sexy @roberto_cavalli animal print look, while @maudeapatow was in prim-and-proper, hot-off-the-runway @celine. In many ways, it was a welcome gear-shift in an age of hyper-curated, method dressing red carpets.
📸 : Jordan Strauss/Presley Ann/Monica Schipper/Mario Anzuoni/Invision/AP/Getty Images

posts.caption:
If Taylor Swift's record-breaking engagement announcement is any indication, Polymarket bets, group chats and neighborhood wagers are undoubtedly sprouting up around the world to guess what she might wear for her upcoming wedding to Travis Kelce.
But Kelce is no fashion slouch himself. A former GQ cover star, the Kansas City Chiefs player has helped spread the tunnel fit phenomenon with his vintage-inflected suits and goofy statement print pieces — helping to rebrand the logistical walkway into a surprising fashion moment for sporting stars. Kelce's tailoring choices on the day Swift walks down the aisle will certainly make news, too.
This week, a clue appeared in the form of a new partnership between Kelce and @tommyhilfiger. It suggests that, just as Swift wore American fashion hero @ralphlauren for her viral engagement photos on Instagram, Kelce may integrate a patriotic great into his matrimonial trousseau. On Monday, Tommy Hilfiger announced that Kelce will join the label as both a global brand ambassador and a creative collaborator.
Tap the link in bio for more.
📸 : Christopher Polk/Billboard/Getty Images, Courtesy of Tommy Hilfiger

posts.caption:
Karl Lagerfeld, who was known to have a portfolio of lavish homes, once said: "The most beautiful house is always the next one."
Your next house could be this graceful French mansion, previously owned by both the late German designer and by European royalty.
The seven-bedroom property, situated less than an hour from Paris, is on sale for 2.7 million euros ($3.1 million).
According to the property listing with realtor PYLA Paris, Manoir du Mée "is an elegant 18th-century residence" that previously belonged to Lagerfeld and then to Princess Caroline of Monaco.
Lagerfeld, who died in Paris in 2019, owned the property for at least a decade until 1998. @chanelofficial staged photo shoots there during that time, including one featuring French supermodel @inesdelafressangeofficial.
Princess Caroline and her family later lived there for six years, during which time her children attended a local school. Her daughter, Charlotte, drew on the walls upstairs and some of that artwork still remains, according to Alexis Feyfant, owner of real estate agency PYLA Paris.
The house was built in 1749 for a notable local family, the Faguiers, Feyfant told CNN. He said another distinguished resident was Renée Saint-Cyr, a French film actress who made her name in the 1930s.
📸 : Matteo Merea/PylaParis.fr
posts.alt_text: Photo by CNN Style on April 02, 2026. May be an image of the Cotswolds and text that says '+ ወኖረ CNN'.

posts.caption:
Victoria Beckham has always kept it pretty simple and that’s exactly why it works !
It’s the fit more than anything. The pants always sit just right, slightly tailored but never overdone. Same with the bags and the oversized sunglasses, they’re consistent, almost like a uniform.
Nothing feels forced or trend-driven. Just clean, put-together, and a bit detached in the best way.
She figured out her look early on and just stuck to it.

posts.caption:
In The Dreamers, the Styling doesn’t feel styled. It feels accidental. Intimate. Almost intrusive.
Eva Green moves between innocence and provocation in sheer slips and soft knits, while Louis Garrel defines effortless chaos with unbuttoned shirts and undone tailoring. Michael Pitt slowly loses structure, his wardrobe dissolving into theirs.
Nothing is polished. Nothing is forced.
– sheer fabrics
– oversized shirts
– bare skin as part of the look
– muted, faded tones
It’s not fashion. It’s a mood.
posts.alt_text: Photo by CULT MAGAZINEⓇ on April 17, 2026. May be an image of text.

posts.caption:
The Versace Fall/Winter 2009 show had a more controlled, grown-up vibe while still keeping that signature sexy energy. There were lots of metallic shades like silver and dark blue, with sleek, body-hugging dresses and sharper pieces like biker jackets and cargos. It felt less flashy than usual, but still very confident.
The evening looks leaned into goddess territory with long, draped gowns and subtle sparkle, giving it a soft but powerful finish.
Honestly, I thought the collection was really cool and sexy — but the makeup was my absolute highlight. The dark, smoky eyes with hints of blue made everything feel hottt !

posts.caption:
In the 1960s, Pierre Cardin emerged as a pioneering figure of fashion futurism, redefining the visual language of an entire generation. His work introduced a radical break from traditional tailoring, replacing it with geometric forms, synthetic materials, and space-age silhouettes that reflected the era’s fascination with technology and progress.
Through sharp lines, experimental fabrics such as vinyl and plexiglass, and bold, graphic compositions, he constructed a new aesthetic that was less about the body and more about form and concept. His designs-ranging from sculptural dresses to androgynous, streamlined menswear, aligned with a broader cultural shift toward modernity and youth-driven identity. ￼
Rather than following fashion, Cardin projected it forward. His vision did not simply reflect the 1960s—it actively shaped its perception of the future, establishing a visual code that would influence designers and define the decade’s cultural imagination.
posts.alt_text: Photo by CULT MAGAZINEⓇ on April 15, 2026. May be an image of standing, costume, turtleneck, headscarf and text that says 'His Style defined α whole Generation... CULT®'.

posts.caption:
In the 2000s, Dior advertising was defined by sharp visuals and controlled sensuality under the direction of John Galliano, the creative director at the time. The campaigns combined high-gloss skin, strong poses, and precise styling.
Images ranged from intimate, sunlit shots with minimal denim looks and tinted sunglasses to more structured compositions with heavy textures, oversized frames, and rigid silhouettes. Softer elements like flowing gowns appeared, but were always presented with clarity and restraint.
The overall tone remained cool, polished, and deliberate.
posts.alt_text: Photo by CULT MAGAZINEⓇ on April 14, 2026. May be an image of magazine and text that says 'From the Archives: Dior Ad's in the 2000s... CULT'.

posts.caption:
Designers at work are often less about glamour than people expect. It’s quiet focus, scattered sketches, fabric samples, half-finished ideas.
There is something inherently inspiring about that space. A desk covered in references, a chair pulled in too close, a wall slowly turning into a process rather than a presentation.
It’s not about perfection in the moment, it’s about building it, piece by piece.

posts.caption:
The longest reign in fashion history just ended.
Hermes is giving Grace Wales Bonner the creative director role for menswear replacing Veronique Nichanian who held the position for 37 years. That is the longest tenure of ANY creative director in luxury fashion history. Nichanian will stage her final show this month.
Wales Bonner is a genius. Her eponymous label blends African diaspora culture with European tailoring in ways nobody else has figured out. She won the LVMH Prize in 2016 and has collaborated with adidas on some of the most sought after sneakers of the last 5 years. Now she gets the biggest menswear job in luxury.
Hermes is being patient. Wales Bonner's debut show is not expected until January 2027.
Is Grace Wales Bonner the right pick for Hermes menswear?
[hermes menswear creative director, grace wales bonner hermes, veronique nichanian retirement, luxury fashion director]
#hermes #gracewaleshonner #culturecircle

posts.caption: I mean yeah ‘everything else’ as in what you and many other culture and music journals cover. There is a music scene, there is a music industry. Many acts in the music scene (mage tears, sign crushes motorist, teen suicide, flatsound, salvia palth to name a few) can garner tens of millions of plays but receive no coverage, festival opportunities or award nominations, but acts with the right PR, agency, manager without a significant fanbase will get article after article and opportunity after oppertunity until the public are convinced they’re relevant. The fact is this coming out is a comment on the music industry but not the music scene, where plentiful acts are making careers and have thriving fanbase but won’t be covered or acknowledged by the industry because they exist on the outskirts of it and because they don’t have the same privileges many of the acts coming from positions of privilege have. Maybe it’s down to the culture journals to actually start sending our journalists and covering acts with organic growth rather than just rewriting press releases.

posts.caption:
#TheNewDiamondOrder 💎
The diamond industry isn’t being disrupted.
It’s being restructured.
Lab-grown diamonds have changed the game:
Pricing is no longer anchored in scarcity
Supply is no longer limited by geology
Consumers are no longer buying the same story
And the biggest shift?
👉 Diamonds are now competing with… diamonds.
This has created a clear divide:
✨ Natural = legacy, rarity, status
✨ Lab-grown = accessibility, size, smart value
For retailers and brands, this is massive:
Lab-grown = higher agility & better margins
Natural = stronger storytelling & luxury positioning
Meanwhile, Gen Z is rewriting the rulebook:
They don’t just ask “how it looks”
They ask “what it stands for”
Which means:
👉 Transparency, traceability & ESG are no longer optional.
The real insight?
This isn’t a product war.
It’s a positioning war.
#DiamondWorld #DWMagazine #NaturalDiamonds #LabGrownDiamonds

posts.caption:
The global diamond trade is entering a new phase — and India is right at the center of it.
With the EU–India FTA unlocking zero-duty access to one of the world’s largest jewellery markets, and the US tariff reset bringing partial relief, exporters are navigating both opportunity and uncertainty at the same time.
But here’s the real shift:
This is no longer a volume game.
Margins, compliance, storytelling, and agility are becoming the new competitive edge.
What this means for the industry:
• Europe is opening doors — but with stricter rules
• The US remains critical — but unpredictable
• Value-led diamonds are gaining importance
• Market diversification is no longer optional
The next growth phase will belong to businesses that can balance scale with strategy.
Save this if you’re planning your next export move.
#DiamondWorld #DWMagazine #DiamondIndustry #GJEPC #GlobalTrade
posts.alt_text: Photo by Diamond World on April 09, 2026. May be a cartoon of poster, magazine and text that says 'DW DAMONDWORLAD EU-India FTA EU-IndiaFTA&US FTA&US & US Tariff Pact A Turning Point for the Diamond Industry'.

Because behind every polished diamond lies an industry built on craft, science, and trust.

posts.caption:
Pete Hegseth quoted a ‘Pulp Fiction’ monologue like it was scripture during a Pentagon prayer service on Wednesday, citing the iconic speech hitman Jules (Samuel L. Jackson) delivers before his victims get whacked.
“They call it CSAR 25:17, which I think is meant to reflect Ezekiel 25:17,” the Secretary of War said. “It reads — and pray with me please — ‘the path of the downed aviator is beset on all sides by the inequities of the selfish and the tyranny of evil men.”
“Blessed is he who in the name of camaraderie and duty shepherds the lost through the valley of darkness, for he is truly his brother’s keeper and the finder of lost children. And I will strike down upon thee with great vengeance and furious anger those who attempt to capture and destroy my brother, and you will know my call sign is Sandy 1 when I lay my vengeance upon thee. Amen.”
In reality, Ezekiel 25:17 is much more brief. “And I will execute great vengeance upon them with furious rebukes; and they shall know that I am the Lord, when I shall lay my vengeance upon them.”
Welp, what’s his next speech going to lift from? Betting on ‘Braveheart.’
#pulpfiction #prayer #news #reels #threads

Cause I've been blastin and laughin so long that even my momma thinks that my mind is gone..."'.

posts.caption:
This week, Massimo Dutti unveils their Limited Edition Spring/Summer ‘26 collection with an immersive retail experience in Paris. To highlight the collection, the brand has collaborated with a handful of artisans and tastemakers from an array of practices spanning culinary, visual, and literary arts.
While the pop-up features apparel, Massimo Dutti has brought an eye-catching roster of collaborators to compliment the collection. Galerie Jacques Lacoste and Galerie Patrick Seguin create an aesthetically precise and artistically fluid space, imbued with the sophistication of the brand. Librairie Yvon Lambert, the Parisian art bookstore beloved by the coolest girl you follow on IG, supplies an editorial perspective within a photo-worthy reading nook, to be enjoyed alongside coffee by the chicest of Parisian coffee shops, Café Nuances.
Of course, the grand opening will be enviably stylish as well with Chef Rose Chalalai supplying a culinary angle for the evening, and florist Nina Charles will punctuate the space with her minimalistic yet moody arrangements.
Visitors will have the opportunity to shop the Spring/Summer ‘26 collection, which is populated by sophisticated staple pieces that are as refined and elegant as they are relaxed and utterly versatile. Languid trousers pair effortlessly with cotton linen silk tops. Thoughtfully draped dresses and skirts beg to be worn on warm summer days and nights. And the collection is anchored by a selection of timeless leather statement pieces in Nappa leather and sheepskin suede.
Massimo Dutti’s immersive approach reflects retail’s larger shift toward experiential offerings that don’t just give customers the opportunity to shop, but demonstrates a brand’s total value proposition in terms of lifestyle, culture, community, and enrichment. The space gracefully aligns Massimo Dutti with contemporary arts and culture pillars at a time when similar brands are grasping to engage in new ways with their customers while appealing to an increasingly upmarket clientele. The pop up will take place from April 17th and 27th at 7 Rue Froissart.

posts.caption:
Lululemon, the workout brand that is famous for its rampant racism, controversial see-through leggings, and murder case that instantly became a true crime classic, is now under investigation in Texas for potentially using “forever chemicals” in its apparel. The investigation was opened by Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton on Monday.
“I will not allow any corporation to sell harmful, toxic materials to consumers at a premium price under the guise of wellness and sustainability,” reads Paxton’s press release.
This isn’t the first time Lululemon has faced such allegations; Polyfluoroalkyls, commonly known as PFA’s, have been found in Lululemon products before. However, the company claims that they “phased out the substance” in 2023.
PFA’s are referred to as “forever chemicals” due to their extreme resistance to degradation, remaining intact in the environment for thousands of years. According to the EPA, we don’t yet know exactly how harmful they are to the human body, but they have been linked to reduced fertility and cancer and are considered highly toxic.
And then there is Ken Paxton who can also be considered highly toxic. He is a MAGA darling who has opened investigations on Tylenol for failing to warn pregnant women that the drug could cause autism in unborn children, and on parents who sought out gender-affirming care for their transgender children. He has been marred by scandals throughout his career, and he remains loyal to the Trump administration and to RFK’s MAHA initiatives. His press release concerning Lululemon is vague about why this investigation is being launched now, citing only “emerging research and consumer concerns.”
What is clear, here, is that both entities are working to send the message that they care about the wellness of their customers/constituents even though their track records tell a different tale. Lululemon and Paxton are each toxic in their own respect, each deserving their downfalls in equal measure. So, in a weird way, we’re happy they found each other, and we look forward to watching how their feud plays out.
#lululemon #kenpaxton #wellness

posts.caption:
@Coach is making a case for the WNBA that fashion’s next real front row is courtside seats. At the 2026 WNBA Draft in New York, the brand, who is the official handbag partner of the league and sponsors its Orange Carpet, dressed incoming players in custom looks designed by Stuart Vevers.
Azzi Fudd, Lauren Betts, Kiki Rice, Gabriela Jaquez, and Raven Johnson were each outfitted in pieces inspired by the codes of the brand’s Fall 2026 collection, like film noir romance and deconstructed tailoring, that were reworked through a collaborative process to reflect each athlete’s personal style.
The result lands somewhere between traditional draft-night polish and something more directional. Repurposed materials give subtle nods to ‘90s grunge and 1940s glamour, like slip dresses with slits, leather with “loveworn” finish, and tailored pieces with inside-out construction details.
The timing isn’t incidental. The WNBA has seen a surge in visibility in recent seasons, with rising attendance, record viewership, and a new generation of players drawing attention well beyond the court. As that audience grows, so does the visibility of the space around it, the arrivals, tunnel walks, and now the Orange Carpet.
For brands, sports are no longer just a sponsorship category but an increasingly viable runway: a place where personal style, performance, and cultural relevance converge in real time, and Coach is wise to have built their multi-year partnership with the league when they did.
#CoachPartner #coach #fashion #WNBA

posts.caption:
Looks like Pope Leo XIV is the target of Trump’s latest crashout per his Truth Social account, which includes an AI-generated image of himself seemingly as Jesus Christ.
Trump’s meltdown seems to trace back to the US-born pope’s criticism of the war against Iran, and more specifically the president’s statement last week threatening to end Iran’s “whole civilization.”
“Leo should get his act together as Pope, use Common Sense, stop catering to the Radical Left, and focus on being a Great Pope, not a Politician,” Trump posted, adding Pope Leo is also “weak on crime” (???)
Over the weekend, Pope Leo held a prayer vigil for peace and spoke about the “delusion of omnipotence that is becoming increasingly unpredictable and aggressive,” without naming the president or any other leaders.
When reporters pressed him Monday about Trump’s messaging, Pope Leo stated he was “not afraid of the Trump administration or of speaking out loudly about the message of the Gospel, which is what the Church works for.”
Trump pulled 55 percent of the Catholic vote in the 2026 election, per Pew Research Center, expanding his base from previous runs.
At this point, does he even have a line left to cross for his base?​​
#wtf #news #threads

posts.caption:
You know how a lot of collections look great on the runway but just don’t translate into real personal style, don’t feel wearable, don’t feel desirable beyond the show, this is the opposite of that, and more and more I’m noticing younger designers pushing craft into a space that feels genuinely international, not “global” as a buzzword but actually wearable across countries and contexts, nothing feels stale, it actually feels current, and we use the word modern a lot but this really feels like it, it reads like ready-to-wear but a more evolved version of it, it doesn’t feel like something you need to carry or protect, you can just throw it on with jeans and step out, it’s light, wearable, easy in the best way, and this brand has completely nailed that.
The brand is @cdk_gyencha, CDK standing for ChanDriKa and GYENCHA meaning ornament, and while the story is strong, what really hits is how it shows up in the clothes, because this return to Bhutan after two years doesn’t just stay sentimental, it feels like a real reconnection with people, place, and memory without getting heavy, and you can see that even in the casting. The show is styled by @dhfranklin, who works with the brand to bring Bhutanese handweaving into a contemporary, minimal, slightly experimental space that honours the roots without freezing them, making it feel current and, most importantly, desirable.

posts.caption: The SK-II launch in India means a few things. First, it is the end of constant outsourcing. No more begging NRI cousins or paying heavy shipping charges just for a restock. posts.caption:
#ELLECoverStar: There are artists who arrive with noise, and then there are artists like Zayn Malik (@zayn), the kind who never really needed to raise his voice to command attention. Some people have presence; Malik has always had pull. Fifteen years after his debut, and nearly a decade into his solo career, Malik returns to ELLE India, where he first made history in 2018 with his first-ever Indian magazine cover. There’s no forced “full circle” narrative here, no overworked nostalgia. If anything, this moment feels less like a return and more like a continuation — the same artist, only more settled into himself. “I don’t feel like I’m at such a different place in my career,” he says. “I feel like I’m at a different place and point as a human; I’m developing, growing, and learning from life experiences.” That distinction feels important. The artistry was never in question. If anything, it is the life around it that now feels more fully aligned. Head to the 🔗in bio to read the full conversation with our cover star.

posts.caption:
@oliviadeano is everywhere—and for good reason.
Since the September release of her hit sophomore album, ‘The Art of Loving,’ the British songstress has been riding a serious wave of momentum on both sides of the pond. Add in a Grammy win, standout live performances, a @cartier campaign, and critical raves, and it’s clear: Dean’s starpower is undeniable. As she prepares to kick off the album’s sold-out world tour, Dean is trying her best to soak it all in—and define success on her own terms.
In ELLE’s Women in Music cover story, @sylviaobell chats with the Grammy winner about navigating public opinion in her newfound spotlight, the wisdom the women in her life have imparted on her, and the precedent she hopes to set with her upcoming tour: “I don’t think you need to be someone who’s got loads of money to enjoy your favorite album.” Read the full interview at the link in bio.

posts.caption:
No, it's not just you: @euphoria looks different in its third season. When the series premiered in 2019, beauty lovers across the globe painted their eyelids purple, pressed pearls along their lash lines, and traced glitter tears down their cheekbones; it was a literal beacon of light in an otherwise dark world. But upon its return, something is shifting. The color is dialed down. The sparkle is more strategic. The chaos, while still present, feels contained.
After a five-year time jump, #Euphoria’s iconic makeup hasn’t lost its edge; instead, it’s grown up alongside its characters. It's what @donni.davy—the show's makeup artist and the architect behind the original sparkle-drenched, high-gloss phenomenon—describes as “glam as armor,” a way for the characters to present the most powerful, “feral” version of themselves. At the link in bio, Davy takes us inside the thinking behind season 3’s beauty transformations, and why, this time around, less might actually say more.

In that spirit, ELLE's 2026 First Class Beauty Awards are here to help. Consider this your travel beauty shortlist, curated by our beauty experts. posts.caption:
“Hi Shakaila we’re xyz producers and we’d love to make a tv show with you” I’ve had a handful of those conversations over the years and they quickly go from 🤩 to 🫩
I used to feel disheartened until I looked around and saw so many examples of people not waiting for permission and instead deciding to get up and make shit happen! So that’s what I decided to do, turn this decades long dream into a reality! I pulled in every favour I had! Turned my friends into producers and show runners, begged and borrowed and you know what, it turned out pretty damn good if I do say so myself 😅
This black history month especially, I’m inspired by the talented Black British women who have shown me that it’s possible to make a mark on the small screen no matter the limitations!
I absolutely loved makeover shows growing up! But as I got older I saw how they were more brutal than beautiful!
Today, fashion psychology shows us just how deeply our clothes impact our mental health, identity, and confidence.
That’s why I’m so excited to share The Wearapy Show my new makeover series that blends fashion psychology, emotional healing and real style transformations.
We help everyday people reclaim their wardrobes after major life shifts: grief, motherhood, identity struggles, and more.
With a team of experts and no judgment, just science-backed guidance and love.
Episodes 1 and 2 launch 26th October on the Fashion is Psychology YouTube Channel. Subscribe now and follow the journey.
#bhm #wearapy #fashionpsychology #makeover

posts.caption:
HUGE NEWS: Good On You has officially rated over 7,000 brands 🎉 This is a major accomplishment, as even more brands—from the small and indie through the large and luxury—are in our Directory.⁠
⁠
What goes into ratings? 🤔 A lot, actually: we have a rigorous, comprehensive system analysing up to 1,000 data points across 100 key issues to arrive at our lowest score (“We Avoid”) to our highest score (“Great”). Ratings are based solely on a brand’s transparent, public reporting that we pair with credible third-party indices, certifications, and standards.⁠
⁠
How do we select brands to rate? Well…with your help 🥹 Many of our ratings come through community requests, shared in comments and messages, which helps us understand which brands to prioritise to meet this moment. Additionally, our retail and ecommerce partners along with brands request ratings too, which is a key means to apply change to the industry from within.⁠
⁠
Another item to celebrate here? Of these rated brands, 1,000 have received our two highest scores, “Good” and “Great”. This is very exciting news, representative of how more brands are doing considering their impact on workers, the planet, and animals. (Even if that also means…6000 brands have a lot more to do to reduce their impact.)⁠
⁠
Here’s to continuing to keep people like you informed while supporting more sustainable brands that are making the difference! Cheers 🥂 Explore our 7,000 ratings at the link too ✨⁠
⁠
#SustainableFashion #FastFashion #ConsciousConsumer #EthicalConsumer #SustainableStyle #ConsciousStyle #GoodOnYou #GoodOnYouApp #EthicalFashion⁠
posts.alt_text: Photo by Good On You: sustainability ratings on January 21, 2026. May be an image of poster.

posts.caption:
Meet the top rated brands of 2025 🎊⁠
⁠
In the past year, we rated more than 1500 brands and are proud to share the ten that received our highest marks, illustrating a commitment to more sustainable practices that protect people, the planet, and animals 💚⁠
⁠
What’s a theme that unites these brands? 👀 Outside of a commitment to reducing their impact, these brands are all small and independent, a reminder of where to look in the industry for leadership and innovation. If you’re looking for a reminder to support small brands this year, this is it.⁠
⁠
Cheers to all these brands, along with all the brands that were rated “Good” and “Great” in the past year! Here’s to continuing to make a difference and for championing a better approach to what we wear. Find the full list at the link—and share which brands you’re hoping to see rated in 2026 in the comments 💬⁠
⁠
#SustainableFashion #FastFashion #SustainableBeauty #EcoBeauty #Beauty #BeautyBrand #ConsciousConsumer #EthicalConsumer #GoodOnYou #GoodOnYouApp #EthicalFashion
posts.alt_text: Photo by Good On You: sustainability ratings on January 08, 2026.

posts.caption:
What can be accomplished in ten years?⁠
⁠
A decade may seem short but, in reflecting on all the work we’ve done in the past decade, we’re in awe of everything that has transpired—and are grateful for the hard work of the current and former team members along with you.⁠
⁠
Whether you’ve shared our posts or have been a partner in this business, the work of creating a more sustainable future isn’t done alone but in partnership with others. These accomplishments are an example of what happens when likeminded people come together in the hopes of a better world 🌍🌎🌏⁠
⁠
Swipe to learn about ten things Good On You has accomplished in the past ten years. Share how you think the sustainable fashion and beauty industry have shifted in the past decade 💚⁠
⁠
#SustainableFashion #FastFashion #SustainableBeauty #EcoBeauty #Beauty #BeautyBrand #ConsciousConsumer #EthicalConsumer #GoodOnYou #GoodOnYouApp #EthicalFashion
posts.alt_text: Photo by Good On You: sustainability ratings on September 03, 2025.

posts.caption:
This is *not* an easy time for smaller, more sustainable brands 😭⁠
⁠
From steep tariffs to economic precarity, retailers skipping payments to fast fashion running prices down, upholding good values in challenging times is indeed a challenge—and small brands are feeling the pressure.⁠
⁠
To have a more sustainable fashion industry, more sustainable fashion has to be supported both by the industry and by consumers. This is why we always say to support top rated brands, shop circularly, and to speak out about your choices, all to turn away from brands whose values are making the survival of sustainability difficult 💔⁠
⁠
How are you navigating this moment, as a shopper, brand, or industry professional? Share in the comments, offering advice or to vent, all in the hopes of supporting the community. Visit the link to find an explainer on the costs of making more sustainable fashion ↑⁠
⁠
#Sustainability #SustainableFashion #EthicalFashion #SmallBrands #SmallBusiness

There has been little relief: here's S what it looks like for small brands now >>> ALEKSANDRA'.

posts.caption:
It’s not just you: conversations about sustainability are disappearing on social media 😢⁠
⁠
What’s happening? A once vibrant movement, inclusive of movements like #WhoMadeMyClothes and #Underconsumption, seems to have been not only been co-opted by big brands but pales when compared to shopping content at large 💔⁠
⁠
We know conversations aren’t weighed equally online, but #Sustainability is a tiny fraction of chatter when compared to something like #Haul or #SHEIN. No wonder real change isn’t happening: the message isn’t being heard, let alone translated into action 😵‍💫⁠
⁠
In a deep dive on this issue, @yemagz explains how the vibe has shifted on social media and what can be done to break through, featuring thoughts from @orsoladecastro and @mrspress. Have you noticed less SustainableFashion conversations on social media? Share in the comments, then read the full story at the link ↑
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Good On You: sustainability ratings on April 16, 2026 tagging @yemagz, and @goodonyou_app. May be an image of magazine and text that says 'The Algorithm Has Abandoned Sustainable Fashion Platforms like Instagram are starting to feel less and less friendly to sustainable conversations. That may not be by accident, as journalist Maggie Zhou finds in a new story >>>'.

posts.caption:
Good news and bad news 😬⁠
⁠
The good? Of the 362 brands we’ve rated so far this year, 28—or 8%—scored our highest ratings of “Good” and “Great”. These brands represent leadership in sustainable fashion and beauty, and should definitely be on your list of brands to watch 💚⁠
⁠
The bad? Our analysts found that a third of brands rated (113!) rated our lowest score, “We Avoid”. These are brands working non-transparently, who are notable for their doing the bare minimum, if anything at all 🫨⁠
⁠
Swipe to learn who these brands are ⏩ Which brands do you want to have rated next? Share in the comments, then explore the full story at the link →↑⁠
⁠
#Shopping #SustainableFashion #Fashion #OOTD #StyleInspiration

posts.caption: I wish my mum had taken breaks, found ways to get away and be herself. Instead she got to the point of a mental breakdown, cheated on my dad and moved 6 hours away from us - only seeing us during school holidays. She tragically passed away in a car accident when I was 10 and I've never had an opportunity to understand from her perspective. However when I finished university I met a mother whilst travelling who's husband had sent her away to get some time to be herself - we had a long chat and both helped each other to understand how necessary it was. I'm now pregnant with my first child and one of my biggest fears is being a repeat of my own mother - and going into it knowing I need to ensure I have time to be me. Thank you for sharing your story - I hope it helps more mothers not to reach breaking point ❤️
posts.alt_text: Photo shared by Grazia UK on April 17, 2026 tagging @ashleylouisejames, and @thejuggleuk. May be an image of one or more people, people standing and text that says ''I went on a solo-trip solo- for my birthday and got mum shamed. I refuse to feel guilty that being a mother isn't my entire identity. ASHLEY JAMES GRAZIA'.

posts.caption:
#LenaDunham’s second memoir, Famesick, is officially on sale – and it’s worth its weight in bombshells. The Girls’ creator leaves little on the cutting room floor, addressing everything from her ex-boyfriend Jack Antonoff’s close relationship with a ‘teen pop star’ to claims about Adam Driver’s ‘aggressive’ behaviour on the set of Girls. Dunham also tackles the statement she wrote defending Girls writer Murray Miller, who was accused of rape in 2017, that got her ‘cancelled’, and details her stint in rehab the following year.
[Driver has not publicly responded to Dunham’s claims. Miller was not charged of rape following a police investigation.]
Will you be reading Famesick? 👇

posts.caption:
Does it get more insane than @kapitalglobal Century Denim jorts? No, it probably does not.
Kapital's Century Denim is perhaps the Japanese brand's single most prized material, a bespoke sashiko-stitched cotton denim often dyed with traditional persimmon or ink treatments. Legend has it, Kapital enlists four separate factories just to produce Century Denim and, as such, it's most commonly utilized for normal trucker jackets and jeans.
But Kapital's giant Century Denim jorts are a deservingly radical departure for a radical textile. They're huge, inspired by the cropped pants worn by rice farmers on Japanese islands, but also clever, with angled pockets securing the goods as front and back pleats shape the volume. Perhaps the best thing about the jorts is how they provide the biggest-ever canvas for Kapital's signature fabric, putting a big ol' slab of earthy denim on full display.
✍🏼 @trasparenti
posts.alt_text: Photo by Highsnobiety Style #HSStyle on April 17, 2026. May be an image of text that says 'KAPI TAL KAPI WORLD'S RAREST DENIM MAKES AN INSANE PAIR OF JORTS H'.

posts.caption:
@tudorwatch just threw a curveball for its 100th birthday, and it lands beautifully.⁠
⁠
Instead of leaning solely on crowd-pleasing Black Bay variations, the brand revived the under-the-radar Monarch, a forgotten early-2000s sports watch. The 2026 version arrives with serious upgrades: a 39mm angular steel case, matching bracelet, and a striking champagne dial with a papyrus-like texture that channels vintage charm. Inside, the new MT5662-2U movement is METAS-certified, pushing the Monarch into Tudor’s top tier at $5,875.⁠
⁠
While fans expected anniversary-safe releases, this left-field comeback stole the spotlight at Watches & Wonders. Blending retro DNA with modern specs, the Monarch proves Tudor’s century mark is not just about looking back, it is about rewriting what deserves a second act.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Highsnobiety Style #HSStyle on April 17, 2026. May be an image of wrist watch and text.
Photo by Highsnobiety Style #HSStyle on April 16, 2026. May be an image of ‎magazine and ‎text that says '‎JEAN WEST'S COWBOYS ARE GIVING WESTWORLD JEANSH SOC aa ج כ Η‎'‎‎.

posts.caption:
@carharttwip is chasing its own ghost.⁠
⁠
Vintage Carhartt is everywhere, prized for its near-indestructible fabric and the way wear and tear only makes it better. That abundance has even sparked a niche of artisans who artificially recreate that perfectly broken-in patina. Now, Carhartt WIP is stepping into the ring, with its Fall/Winter 2026 collection leaning heavily into pre-aged workwear. Think faded hoodies with ghosted logos, paint-splattered jackets, and accessories that look like they’ve survived years on a job site.⁠
⁠
The look mirrors recent hype pieces like Supreme x MM6’s distressed hoodies but at a more accessible price point. Still, the irony lingers: you could achieve a similar effect yourself with some actual wear or a DIY paint job. Either way, Carhartt WIP is embracing the reality that if vintage is king, the best move might be to manufacture the legend.
posts.alt_text: Photo by Highsnobiety Style #HSStyle on April 16, 2026. May be an image of text.

posts.caption:
@ourlegacy WORK SHOP SPORT is not merely clothing for activity. It's clothing for life. Real life!⁠
⁠
WORK SHOP SPORT, a sub-label within Our Legacy's WORK SHOP sub-label, does not limit itself to sport, as its name may imply. It of course is supremely well-suited to activity: its breezy jackets and suave quick-dry cotton shorts work just fine for jogging, golf, hooping, cycling, and dashing to dinner. But these clothes go beyond sportswear. They're as considered as anything in Our Legacy mainline, and even borrow some of the silhouettes. Think of WORK SHOP SPORT as purpose-built athleisure, as easy and as useful as sportswear, just done better.⁠
⁠
"My experience is that 'athleisure' often lacks the performance function," Our Legacy cofounder Jockum Hallin tells us. "And straight-up technical sports pieces, you want them off so you can put on your “normal” clothes. Here, we are trying to create the perfect hybrids."
✍🏼 @trasparenti
posts.alt_text: Photo by Highsnobiety Style #HSStyle on April 16, 2026. May be an image of magazine, tights, sportswear, sports equipment and text that says 'WHY OUR LEGACY WENT ALL-IN ΟΝ SPORT (EXCLUSIVE) Η'.

posts.caption:
@gap turned Coachella into a hoodie playground, and everyone showed up.
Amid the festival’s sea of brand activations, the electric-blue Hoodie House stood out like a mirage of cool, drawing the longest lines all weekend. Inside, fans customized GAP’s iconic hoodies with Coachella-branded logos, patches, metallic charms, and bead details, all heat-pressed into one-of-a-kind keepsakes. The space doubled as a cultural time capsule, decked out with oversized records from past GAP collaborators like Tyla, N.E.R.D, and Madonna, plus Polaroid moments and claw machine goodies to seal the experience.
Beyond the aesthetics, the magic lived in the stories. Festival-goers spoke about comfort, nostalgia, and even post-breakup reinvention through their hoodies, while others leaned into oversized fits inspired by Bieber-core dreams. Whether tied to dance class memories, shared wardrobes, or childhood shopping sprees, the throughline was clear: GAP isn’t just clothing, it’s emotional uniform. And at Coachella, it proved once again that hoodie season never really ends.
✍ @ericacxmpbell

posts.caption:
A$AP Rocky’s @hommemade introduces the "TRASH BAG COUCH" — the rapper's latest furniture piece made in partnership with Harry Nuriev’s @crosbystudios 🗑️⁠
⁠
Comprising bean bag modules constructed and printed to look like a pile of NYC street trash, the couch continues hOMMEMADE’s surrealist design aesthetic. For this seating design, clear, black, red, and blue trash bags appear to be stuffed with plastic containers, crumpled cash, candy packaging, and other miscellaneous objects. ⁠
⁠
The brand debuted another collection at Design Miami 2024, complete with a bird's-eye-view city rug for $18,000 USD and a $13,500 USD Skyline Divider, which takes the cityscape to the next level with towering skyscrapers arranged in foldable panels. How much do you think he’s charging for this one?⁠
⁠
Photo: hOMMMADE⁠

posts.caption:
Summer has a mood for every version of you—and @nidhhiagerwal knows exactly how to wear each one. Whether it’s the soft, golden haze of a slow morning or the unapologetic heat of high noon, she shifts between them the way only someone truly at ease in her own skin can. 🌸
In collaboration with @aurelia_womenswear , this becomes more than a seasonal study—it is a portrait of summer as emotion, texture, and transformation.
Light changes, moods shift, and she moves through it all with the kind of effortless cool that makes you want to rethink your entire wardrobe.
From dewy and dreamy to bold and sun-kissed, this is summer dressing as self-expression—textured, emotional, and completely her own.✨

But off-screen things are less grim: Thurman is energized by her work and looking forward to the possibilities of her next chapter—one where she’s making space for new roles, new interests, and more time for herself: “I think the choice to be happy is one that should be made regardless of any conditions.”

posts.caption:
Adarsh Gourav’s (@gouravadarsh) rise is a masterclass in range. From playing a young Shah Rukh Khan in ‘My Name is Khan’ to early work in Clean Shaven, and Madly alongside Radhika Apte, he’s built one of the most compelling filmographies of his generation. His breakthrough as Balram in The White Tiger earned a BAFTA nomination, and he’s since transformed across roles, from Kho Gaye Hum Kahan to Superboys of Malegaon and Alien: Earth.
Now, with Tu Yaa Main alongside Shanaya Kapoor, Gourav proves one thing… wherever the story goes, he makes it impossible to look away. We step into the dreamer and shapeshifter’s world for our April cover.

posts.caption:
Man of the Moment — Yashasvi Jaiswal (@yashasvijaiswal28) continues to rise, and we’re here for every second of it.
From dreaming of centuries in blue to turning those visions into reality and now hitting it out of the park as the opener for Rajasthan Royal (@rajasthanroyals) in IPL, his journey is the very definition of hard work meeting opportunity. Powered as much by an unshakable mindset as by his brilliance on the pitch, our cover star opens up about the drive, discipline, and hunger that fuel his remarkable ascent, “I don’t know where I’m going to go, but I want to enjoy the journey.”
Starting out as a cricketing prodigy, now 24, he’s all about keeping his head in the game, stopping every now and then, to savour the simple joys of life. And maybe that is the secret sauce to success, one game at a time.
Here’s raising a toast to the ‘player of the match’- Yashasvi Jaiswal!

posts.caption:
It’s difficult to place Adarsh Gourav (@gouravadarsh). Who is he when the camera isn’t rolling? Who is he when it is? A natural born actor, a star on the rise, or someone who leads life without a script?
“I have always struggled with this question of how do I describe myself. While I am self-aware, I have always found it hard. I don’t know if I want to exist in any category or any archetype. I’m just trying to be honest to myself and honest to the choices that I make. I’m clear about the things that I want to do,” he tells us in our cover interview.

posts.caption:
There’s a certain air of romance about Adarsh Gourav (@gouravadarsh). It follows him as he saunters into the coffee shop cum bookstore, we have ready to capture that elusive energy. There’s a romance in the way he flips through a book, sipping on his coffee. It’s in the way he likes his hair tousled, and the cheeky half smirk he breaks into now and then. Often, I catch him lost in thought, perhaps in a dream like stupor. After all, he’s a dreamer he tells me.
“I feel like I have been somebody who’s always lived a little bit more in my daydreams than in my reality. I dream a lot and I also get a lot of dreams at night, but I also keep getting lost in my own imagination a lot of times,” he says.

posts.caption:
Introducing L’OFFICIEL’s cover star, @penelopecruzoficial!
The actor found her calling at a young age, imagining herself in other worlds and pretending to be someone else. What began as a childhood game became a lifelong pursuit—not to escape, but to understand people and the world around her.
Cruz still brings that same curiosity and depth to her work today. Up next, she’ll star opposite Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale in Maggie Gyllenhaal’s ‘The Bride!’ Inspired by James Whale’s 1935 film ‘The Bride of Frankenstein,’ a bold gothic thriller casts Cruz as a detective hunting real-life monsters in the 1930s while also struggling for recognition in a male-dominated field. “She has to fight very hard,” Cruz tells L’OFFICIEL. “The same way women everywhere, in every profession, still have to work extremely hard to be valued.”
It’s a sentiment Cruz knows well. After decades of working non-stop—at one point making four films a year—the Oscar winner and longtime Chanel muse is now more selective with her roles. “I never regret the things that I say ‘No’ to,” she says. “Especially if the reason is just to be with my family and to raise my kids in a very present way.”
Visit the link in our bio for more about Cruz’s intentional approach to her work, her relationship with fame, her role in ‘The Bride!’, and more.

posts.caption:
Nuovo capitolo, stessa intensità. @penelopecruzoficial è la nostra nuova cover star e torna sullo schermo con The Bride!, diretto da Maggie Gyllenhaal.
Più di trent’anni di carriera, iniziata a 17 anni con Jamón Jamón di Bigas Luna, sette film con Pedro Almodóvar, un Oscar per Vicky Cristina Barcelona. Europa e Stati Uniti come due case, lo sguardo sempre acceso sul presente.
Nell’intervista a L’OFFICIEL, Penélope racconta l’incontro con Maggie e la nascita di un progetto che vibra di verità:
«Conosco Maggie da molti anni e abbiamo sempre desiderato lavorare insieme. Ho adorato il suo primo film, The Lost Daughter, tratto da un libro di Elena Ferrante. Lavorare con lei è stata una bellissima esperienza. Ha realizzato un film molto speciale, profondo, a tantissimi livelli. Visivamente attraente, molto rock and roll. C’è tanta verità».
Cinema che scava, immagini che restano. Penélope, come sempre, va dritta al cuore.

posts.caption:
Kendall Jenner e Jacob Elordi. Nessuna conferma, nessuna dichiarazione, nessuna ufficialità, eppure il loro nome insieme funziona già come immaginario: cool, super chiacchierato.
Forse perché entrambi hanno quella presenza che trasforma tutto in immagine. E forse anche perché il legame con #BottegaVeneta rende tutto ancora più credibile sul piano estetico: Jacob Elordi è ambassador della maison, mentre Kendall Jenner fa da tempo parte di quell’universo visivo preciso, classy e desiderabile. Che sia davvero qualcosa o solo la proiezione perfetta del fashion internet, una cosa è certa: l’idea piace. E parecchio.

Negli anni ’50, Balenciaga fece del taffetà di seta uno dei suoi codici più potenti, usandolo per costruire volumi scultorei e silhouette quasi architettoniche. Anche il rosso, nella sua visione, non era mai solo un colore, ma un segno visivo assoluto, intenso, drammatico, impossibile da ignorare. Un immaginario che riaffiora anche negli archivi de L’OFFICIEL, dalle cover del 1950, Issue 343 e del 1951, Issue 349, dove quella stessa idea di couture scenografica era già impressa sulla pagina.

posts.caption:
Introducing our cover star, Grace Burns!
The model steps into the spotlight in standout looks from the spring runways in Paris, where American sportswear’s influence was impossible to miss. But even as she follows in the footsteps of her mother, the legendary Christy Turlington Burns (@cturlington), @graciebrns wasn’t always set on a career in fashion.
“For as long as I can remember, I worried about when, if ever, I would find my passion or purpose, because that’s what I saw in my parents,” she tells L’OFFICIEL. Also a photographer, writer, and actor, she’s equally invested in building a creative life that feels expansive and honest, exploring different mediums while still discovering what feels most like her. Head to the link in our bio to hear more from Burns, and to see her in some of the season’s best American sportswear–inspired looks from across the Atlantic.

posts.caption:
Introducing L’OFFICIEL’s cover star, @penelopecruzoficial!
The actor found her calling at a young age, imagining herself in other worlds and pretending to be someone else. What began as a childhood game became a lifelong pursuit—not to escape, but to understand people and the world around her.
Cruz still brings that same curiosity and depth to her work today. Up next, she’ll star opposite Jessie Buckley and Christian Bale in Maggie Gyllenhaal’s ‘The Bride!’ Inspired by James Whale’s 1935 film ‘The Bride of Frankenstein,’ a bold gothic thriller casts Cruz as a detective hunting real-life monsters in the 1930s while also struggling for recognition in a male-dominated field. “She has to fight very hard,” Cruz tells L’OFFICIEL. “The same way women everywhere, in every profession, still have to work extremely hard to be valued.”
It’s a sentiment Cruz knows well. After decades of working non-stop—at one point making four films a year—the Oscar winner and longtime Chanel muse is now more selective with her roles. “I never regret the things that I say ‘No’ to,” she says. “Especially if the reason is just to be with my family and to raise my kids in a very present way.”
Visit the link in our bio for more about Cruz’s intentional approach to her work, her relationship with fame, her role in ‘The Bride!’, and more.

posts.caption:
#ManifestCover
From engineered gym run-ins to a romance that unfolded at its own pace, Allu Sirish (@allusirish) and Nayanika Reddy’s (@nayanika_reddy) story feels less like a whirlwind and more like a rhythm built over time.
“It wasn’t like we suddenly started going on back-to-back dates. It was more of a slow-burn romance,” says Sirish, recalling how a series of chance meetings — and one very intentional gym switch — finally brought them together.
For Nayanika, the turning point was simpler.
“I realised how easy it felt to be around him. Our conversations were natural, and there was always a sense of comfort. Over time, it became clear that we were building something meaningful,” she shares.
Just days before their wedding, the couple comes together for their first editorial shoot, speaking to Manifest about love that grew organically, families that were always one step ahead, and why the most meaningful relationships don’t need grand gestures to feel certain.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full cover story from the link in bio.

posts.caption:
#ManifestDigitalCover | The Shefali Shah Effect
For over three decades, Shefali Shah (@shefalishahofficial) has built a career on instinct, honesty, and an unwavering refusal to fit into boxes. From the unforgettable Pyari in Satya to the formidable DCP Vartika Chaturvedi in Delhi Crime, her performances have never relied on noise — only truth.
“A few years ago, if someone told me a woman in her mid-forties would lead a show, I would have laughed. And if they said it would be me, I would have laughed louder,” she says.
In our latest digital cover, the actor reflects on evolving with time, finding freedom in flawed characters, 25 years of marriage, and why success today means something far quieter than fame.
Our latest issue is now live on Magzter. Read the full story from the link in bio.

posts.caption:
Bridgerton isn’t just a romantic period drama, it’s one of Netflix’s most lavish productions. The series reportedly carries a staggering budget of around $168 million, placing it among the most expensive shows created by the streaming giant.
Each episode is estimated to cost around $7 million, funding the show’s extravagant costumes, grand sets, and cinematic storytelling that recreates Regency-era London. The scale of production is what gives the series its signature opulence and visual richness.
The spending doesn’t stop at sets and wardrobes. Reports also reveal impressive cast salaries, with some actors and voice talents earning up to $1 million for their roles, reflecting the show’s massive global success.
From lavish balls to royal romances, Bridgerton proves that luxury storytelling comes with a royal price tag and Netflix is willing to pay for it.
Images: Courtesy of Netflix
#Netflix #Luxurious

posts.caption:
Indian cricketer Jasprit Bumrah was recently seen wearing the ₹36-lakh @JacobAndCo Epic X ‘Sher-e-Punjab’ Edition, a distinctive timepiece that blends high-end watchmaking with strong cultural inspiration.
Created by Jacob & Co as a tribute to Sikh heritage, the watch features a lightweight 44mm titanium case and a skeletonised dial that reveals the intricate mechanics within. The vertical bridges are engraved with meaningful Sikh symbols, including the lion representing courage and leadership, the Khanda symbolising strength and unity, and the sacred Ek Onkar reflecting the belief in one universal God.
The titanium structure is paired with a bold blue honeycomb-textured rubber strap, a colour closely associated with Sikh identity. At its core is the hand-wound JCAM02 movement, offering a 48-hour power reserve while showcasing the watch’s detailed inner workings through the open dial.
Limited to just 50 pieces worldwide, the Sher-e-Punjab Edition honours the legacy of Guru Gobind Singh and the founding of the Khalsa, making it both a luxury watch and a tribute to heritage and resilience. ⌚✨

posts.caption:
Richard Mille has unveiled its latest high-performance timepiece—the RM 55-01—a six-figure watch engineered to withstand extreme shocks while maintaining exceptional mechanical precision.
Inspired by the brand’s long-standing connection to Bubba Watson, the RM 55-01 builds on the legacy of the RM 055, originally designed with the demands of professional golf in mind. This new iteration continues that philosophy, focusing on durability, lightness, and structural integrity for high-impact use.
At its core is the manual-winding Caliber RMUL4, a skeletonized movement designed for efficiency and resilience. Built on a grade 5 titanium baseplate, it is engineered to absorb shocks without compromising accuracy. A free-sprung balance with variable inertia enhances stability, while a double-barrel system ensures consistent torque delivery and reliable timekeeping.
Available in Carbon TPT, White Quartz TPT, and Grey Quartz TPT, the RM 55-01 uses advanced composite materials that combine exceptional strength with a remarkably lightweight feel.
More than a luxury statement, the RM 55-01 is a purpose-built sports watch—demonstrating how high-end watchmaking can prioritize real-world performance as much as prestige.
@RichardMille #RichardMille #Luxurious

posts.caption:
Jaeger-LeCoultre has just unveiled one of its most artistic Reverso watches yet. Inspired by Japanese artist Katsushika Hokusai, these pieces turn a watch into a tiny work of art. It’s less about time, and more about storytelling on the wrist.
Each watch features hand-painted enamel artwork on the back. These designs recreate Hokusai’s famous waterfall scenes in incredible detail. Every piece takes dozens of hours to complete, making each one extremely rare.
What makes it special is the Reverso’s flip design. One side shows a clean, elegant dial, while the other reveals the artwork. It’s like owning two watches in one, simple on the front, artistic on the back.
With this release, Jaeger-LeCoultre is showing how luxury is evolving. It’s no longer just about mechanics, but about art and craftsmanship. And this watch brings both together in the most refined way.
Images: @jaegerlecoultre
#HauteHorlogerie #JaegerLeCoultre #WatchCollectors #Luxurious

posts.caption:
BMW is redefining private aviation with its new collaboration with Lufthansa Technik, unveiling a VIP jet concept called The BOW. Instead of designing for one billionaire, this aircraft is built for entire groups like CEOs, sports teams, or artists to travel together in luxury. It turns a standard jet into a shared, high-end flying experience.
At its core, the concept strips down a typical Airbus A321 from over 200 seats to just around 28 passengers. In place of rows, you get private suites, lounge spaces, and even a club-style bar. The idea is simple, fewer people, more space and a completely elevated way to travel.
What makes it different is the design philosophy. Instead of separate compartments, the cabin flows like one continuous living space, almost like a luxury home in the sky. Every element, from lighting to curved architecture, is designed to feel seamless, calm and immersive.
More than just a jet, this is a shift in how luxury travel is evolving. It’s no longer about individual ownership, but about shared exclusivity and experience. With The BOW, BMW is quietly showing that the future of private aviation is less about flying alone, and more about traveling together, in style.
Images: Courtesy of @Lufthansa
#LuxuryTravel #PrivateJet #Luxurious

posts.caption:
Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York said in an interview with Hell Gate that he would not attend the Met Gala, a star-studded fund-raiser for the Metropolitan Museum of Art’s fashion wing.
Past leaders of the city have made a point of attending the benefit as a show of support for one of New York’s oldest cultural institutions. Michael Bloomberg, Bill de Blasio and Eric Adams have all walked the museum’s famous staircase alongside celebrities, models, athletes, fashion designers and, increasingly, tech billionaires.
Mamdani’s presence at the gala might have seemed out of place at an event that is widely considered to be the playground of the rich and famous. Elected in November on a socialist platform focused on lowering costs for average New Yorkers, Mamdani has made raising taxes on the wealthy a central element of his political brand. Read more about Mamdani’s decision at the link in our bio. Photo by @angelinakatsanis
